release_atomicity: ordered-but-unspecced: bead->open happens BEFORE WorktreeOps.Remove, so a Remove failure leaves bead=open + worktree still present (orphaned, re-claimable with a stale checkout). The spec names the reversal sequence in Design Decisions but does not spec failure ordering, partial-failure recovery, or whether bd update needs MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN handling / how issues.jsonl re-export is reconciled.
blocking: R2a cwd-deletion footgun UNADDRESSED (qxsy/092 bug class). `release` removes the bead worktree via WorktreeOps.Remove, but the spec never mentions running `release` from INSIDE that worktree, restoring cwd, or chdir-to-root. complete.go already carries an explicit os.Chdir(root) after CompleteBead removes the worktree (spec 092 Req 3c) precisely because the process is stranded in a deleted cwd and subsequent bd subprocesses silently degrade. `release` reintroduces the identical hazard with no mitigation. The R2 AC injects a fake WorktreeOps, so the test suite cannot catch it. The spec must require release to chdir to repo root before any post-removal bd call (cursor rewind, bd update) and state the from-inside-worktree case is supported.

R2d release atomicity/half-state not specced. Reversal order is bead->open (assignee cleared) THEN WorktreeOps.Remove THEN cursor rewind. If Remove fails, bead is already open while the worktree persists -> a re-claimable bead pointing at a leftover, possibly-dirty checkout. Spec should either (i) remove worktree first, set bead open last, or (ii) define explicit partial-failure recovery + a clear error. Also: does the bd update (status/assignee) need MINDSPEC_ALLOW_MAIN-style handling and does it auto-commit/re-export issues.jsonl? Unaddressed.
summary: R1 is design-sound and cleanly implementable: filter the named bead out of the already-fetched ready `items` set (not via --pick); a ready-but-not-items[0] bead composes fine, and a blocked/closed bead is absent from the filtered set so it naturally errors as 'not found/not ready'. Minor non-blocking gap: precedence of positional vs an explicit --pick when both are passed is unspecced. R2b cursor rewind is sound and largely automatic — the cursor/active-bead is DERIVED from bd's single in_progress child (ADR-0023), so setting the bead back to open self-rewinds the derived cursor; 'rewind only when it points at the released bead' is the right semantics (the mindspec_phase metadata cache may need a sync but the resolution is correct). R2c dirty-refuse is detectable via CheckDirtyTree(repoRoot, worktreePath) which already snapshots porcelain at an arbitrary worktree path; note bd's WorktreeRemove is hardcoded --force at the CLI layer, so release's --force is a mindspec-level pre-gate, not a passthrough — worth stating. BLOCKING: (a) the R2 cwd-deletion footgun is the exact qxsy/092 bug class and is wholly unaddressed — release deletes the worktree the user may be standing in, with no chdir-to-root or cwd-restore mentioned, and the fake-WorktreeOps ACs can't catch it; (b) release's bead-open-before-worktree-removal ordering creates an unspecced half-state on partial failure. Both need speccing before approve.
